en.title: Voice input
en.prompt: Using concise and clear language, please edit the following passage to improve its logical flow, eliminate any typographical errors. Be sure to maintain the original meaning of the text. Please begin by editing the following text: [voice input transcript]
en.remark: When making voice recordings, it is often customary to say verbal and intonational words, which can then be converted into written language using ChatGPT to optimise the speech-to-text effect. Additionally, it can also be used to organize disordered text.

related.title: Writing assistant
related.remark: For optimising the grammar, clarity and conciseness of text and improving readability.

related.title: Nature Style Editing
related.remark: Polish according to the style of Nature, or provide a writing style to emulate. Contributed by @Pfyuan77.

metaTitle: Voice Input Optimizer: Polish Spoken Text
metaDescription: Clean up voice-to-text transcriptions: correct errors and improve flow while preserving the speaker's original meaning.

faq.q: When cleaning up a voice-to-text transcript, will it strip my verbal tics too?
faq.a: Yes. The AI defaults to removing filler words like "um," "you know," and "I mean." If you want to keep certain tics as part of your personal voice, say explicitly "keep the tic X as part of my speech rhythm, do not remove it," otherwise the output will feel overly written.

faq.q: Can I clean up a transcript of several thousand words in one go?
faq.a: Not recommended. GPT tends to cut corners or even skip entire middle sections on very long input. Break it into chunks by topic or every 2000 words, clean each piece, then do a final pass over the whole thing for transitions.
